top_findings.summary: Promotes unsafe installation via curl-pipe-bash from an external domain (canifi.com) without verification, risking arbitrary code execution.
Skill claims extensive Apple News integration capabilities but provides no executable code in artifacts, making verification impossible.

spans.why: Claims capabilities: personalized_feed, channel_following, news_plus_access, offline_reading, audio_stories

top_findings.summary: Skill description claims 'system-level Apple ID authentication' and 'credentials never transmitted', but configuration collects raw APPLE_ID and APPLE_PASSWORD environment variables, creating a contradictory and misleading privacy promise.

spans.why: Requires APPLE_ID and APPLE_PASSWORD environment variables

top_findings.summary: Instructs users to store sensitive Apple ID credentials in a local tool (canifi-env) without detailing security controls, access boundaries, or encryption.

spans.why: Instructions: 'canifi-env set APPLE_ID "your_apple_id"' and 'canifi-env set APPLE_PASSWORD "your_password"'

missed.why: No Python, JavaScript, or other executable code files present in artifacts; cannot analyze actual skill implementation or verify credential usage.
External repository mentioned in configuration; may contain source code not included in artifacts, needed to verify implementation.
External script referenced in curl-pipe-bash command; needed to verify download-and-execute payload.
